subject: Re: K3 tests
body:
Hi Andrey,
I see CTF failing if its parameters are too far off. Size of CTF window plays a role, and I aim for starting the fit at 30Å. Would be nice if the fitting range for CTF can be set in Å instead of % of Nyquist.
Sometimes using movie sum works better. All this is why there is the feature to run CTF on just one selected  image in the FFT view: Sometimes these things simply need some playing around before starting processing.
Or has everything been tried but nothing worked at all in this area?
Best,
Wim

> On 10. Apr 2019, at 19:39, Andrey Malyutin <malyutinag@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> Hi Dimitry,
> 
> Looking forward to testing 1.0.7 soon! We just upgraded to a K3 and I have been trying to use Warp with the data. I can get the run going on 1080s. 2 GPUs generally run without crashing, 4 GPUs crash some of the time. However, the issue I'm running into is when providing raw super res frames and binning once. From the averages, it looks like the output is correct, and the image and pixel sizes are correctly adjusted based on the header. However, CTF estimation is not running correctly. There is a clear mismatch in the Overview between the modeled pattern and the rings in the data and the estimated defocus is way off. Have you noticed anything like this in your tests?
> 
> Thank you,
> 
> Andrey
> 
>> On Tuesday, April 9, 2019 at 10:14:11 AM UTC-7, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>> Hi,
>> 
>> as Da noted, K3 data will cause Warp 1.0.6 to run out of memory on most consumer GPUs. The upcoming 1.0.7W fixes that, at least on a 1080 Ti card (I haven't tested 1080 yet). If everything goes ok, the beta will be out tomorrow.
>> 
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>> 
>> 
>>> On Friday, April 5, 2019 at 2:38:16 AM UTC+2, Da Cui wrote:
>>> Hi Ming, 
>>>   I just ran Warp with my K3 data. It only works when I binned the micrographs twice. 
>>> —Da
>>> 
>>> Ming Sun <min...@msg.ucsf.edu>于2019年4月4日 周四下午7:32写道：
>>>> Hi All
>>>> 
>>>> Has anyone tried warp on K3? Any inputs are warm welcome
>>>> 
>>>> Thanks,
>>>> Ming
>>>>
